hero_summary: In practical Arabic explanation, Jaharkah is built from Jins Ajam on Jaharkah (F), followed by Jins Rast on Kardan (C in the upper octave).
short_identity: A bright, large-colored maqam whose Arabic teaching profile begins with Ajam on F and expands upward into Rast on C in the upper register.

quick_facts.upper_jins: Jins Rast on Kardan (C in the upper octave)
quick_facts.character: Bright and stable, with a practical Arabic structure of Ajam below and Rast above
quick_facts.common_start: It begins from Jaharkah (F) by establishing Jins Ajam, then rises until Rast on Kardan becomes clear

sayr.summary: In practical Arabic explanation, Jaharkah is a maqam built from Jins Ajam on Jaharkah (F), followed by Jins Rast on Kardan (C in the upper octave). This is the concise Arabic structure that captures the maqam clearly and directly without needing to rely on more involved Turkish theoretical formulations. The maqam is stable and large-colored in character, though less commonly used than Rast or Bayati.
resting_tones: Jaharkah (F): the tonic and final resting point
Kardan (C in the upper octave): the ghammaz and the place of the upper Rast jins
Intermediate degrees such as Nawa (G) and Husayni (A) may serve as passing or temporary settling tones, but the main structural poles remain Jaharkah and Kardan
motion_notes: Its sayr in Arabic explanation is understood as beginning from Jaharkah (F) with a clear Ajam coloration, then expanding upward until Rast on Kardan becomes audible, before returning to the tonic
Its identity is not captured by a plain scalar reading alone, but by the move from Ajam on F into Rast on C above, then back down to the tonic
A performer should not blur it into a generic major scale on F, because the upper Rast component is part of its Arabic maqamic identity
Some practical discussions note a distinctly popular or folk-like Egyptian flavor in parts of its use
common_path: Reveal Jins Rast on Kardan (C in the upper octave)
Return to Jaharkah with a clear cadence

common_modulations: Jaharkah is close to Ajam in broad color because its lower jins is Ajam, but in practical Arabic explanation it is completed by Rast on Kardan rather than by a single uninterrupted Ajam framework
It differs from Rast because its identity begins from Ajam on F, not from Rast on C, even though Rast appears above
It should not be treated as merely a generic major scale on F, because the presence of the upper Rast layer gives it a specifically Arabic modal organization

examples.title: Independent Jaharkah presentation
examples.note: Amar presents Jaharkah in a dedicated discussion and links it to older performance practice and a clear popular flavor in Egyptian use

reference_recordings.title: Jaharkah presentation context
reference_recordings.note: A practical witness to the maqam’s performance identity
